description:
Goal: make compiler2 source identity an owned quoted/source value, not shared `src/ast::Item` object identity.

Signal:
- compiler2 has a focused source AST / quoted-AST model that can represent items, expressions, patterns, attributes, metadata, spans, and lexical context needed by macro expansion
- source-value equality/revisions are based on compiler2-owned content/identity, not `Rc::ptr_eq` over old AST nodes
- the model can encode the worked examples: `defmacro inc`, `quote/unquote`, `defmodule Helpers`, `import Helpers, only: [twice: 1]`, and item-level `test(:name) do ... end`

Change:
- introduce compiler2-owned source syntax/value modules for quoted AST and source metadata
- define lexical-context metadata for identifiers so macro-authored names and unquoted caller AST can resolve against different namespaces correctly
- define the process-heap allocation/ownership contract for source AST values, including atom/list/tuple/map shapes that match fz runtime values
- replace any new compiler2 source identity decisions that would otherwise depend on shared Rust AST pointer identity

Why:
- a parser fork without an owned source value just recreates the current old-world AST dependency under a different module name
- macro redefinition and expansion must wake facts through honest source revisions
title: Compiler2 source AST: own quoted syntax and lexical-context identity
